meta.title: Original Atoms Practice Question Bank
meta.licenseNote: Original educational questions generated for this project. No copied coaching material or copyrighted question banks.

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Closest Approach?
questions.answer: K = k(2e)(Ze)/r_min
questions.solution: Closest Approach: Energy conservation in head-on alpha scattering. Therefore the correct relation is K = k(2e)(Ze)/r_min.
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Bohr Quantization?
questions.answer: mvr = n h/(2pi)
questions.solution: Bohr Quantization: Allowed angular momentum. Therefore the correct relation is mvr = n h/(2pi).
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Bohr Radius?
questions.answer: r_n = n^2 a0
questions.solution: Bohr Radius: Hydrogen radius grows as n squared. Therefore the correct relation is r_n = n^2 a0.
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Hydrogen Energy?
questions.solution: Hydrogen Energy: Bound-state energy of hydrogen. Therefore the correct relation is E_n = -13.6/n^2 eV.
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Transition Energy?
questions.answer: hf = E_i - E_f
questions.solution: Transition Energy: Photon energy equals level difference. Therefore the correct relation is hf = E_i - E_f.
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Rydberg Formula?
questions.answer: 1/lambda = R(1/n_f^2 - 1/n_i^2)
questions.solution: Rydberg Formula: Hydrogen spectral wavelengths. Therefore the correct relation is 1/lambda = R(1/n_f^2 - 1/n_i^2).
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Lyman Series?
questions.answer: n_f = 1
questions.solution: Lyman Series: Transitions ending at ground state. Therefore the correct relation is n_f = 1.
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Balmer Series?
questions.answer: n_f = 2
questions.solution: Balmer Series: Transitions ending at second level. Therefore the correct relation is n_f = 2.
questions.commonTrap: Not all Balmer lines visible to eye.
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Paschen Series?
questions.answer: n_f = 3
questions.solution: Paschen Series: Infrared hydrogen series. Therefore the correct relation is n_f = 3.
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Ionization Energy?
questions.answer: E_ion = 13.6/n^2 eV
questions.solution: Ionization Energy: Energy needed to remove electron from level n. Therefore the correct relation is E_ion = 13.6/n^2 eV.
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable

questions.question: Which relation correctly represents Number of Spectral Lines?
questions.solution: Number of Spectral Lines: Possible downward transitions among n levels. Therefore the correct relation is N = n(n-1)/2.
questions.shortcut: Draw the model first, then choose the formula.
options: It is independent of the main variable

questions.solution: Hydrogen-like Energy: One-electron ion energy. Therefore the correct relation is E_n = -13.6 Z^2/n^2 eV.
